slice: P1 Herdr workspace lease cleanup gate

commands_run.command: uv run ruff check --select F,I src/tau_coding/herdr_cleanup.py src/tau_coding/provider_dag_poc.py src/tau_coding/cli.py tests/test_herdr_cleanup.py tests/test_provider_pane_poc.py
uv run pytest tests/test_herdr_cleanup.py tests/test_provider_pane_poc.py tests/test_run_status.py -q

uv run pytest tests/test_herdr_cleanup.py tests/test_provider_pane_poc.py tests/test_run_status.py tests/test_real_world_sanity_runner.py tests/test_generic_provider_adapter.py tests/test_provider_lifecycle.py -q

proves: tau.herdr_cleanup_receipt.v1 apply mode blocks before Herdr close commands when a workspace lease is missing.
tau.herdr_workspace_lease.v1 metadata is checked for schema, owner, run_id binding, cleanup_policy, covered workspace ids, and expiry.
Cleanup apply does not invoke Herdr close/get when the lease is missing, expired, unreadable, or does not cover all workspace candidates.
Provider DAG cleanup writes a run-owned herdr-workspace-lease.json and passes it into Herdr cleanup.
The tau herdr-cleanup CLI accepts --workspace-lease and returns a project-agent-readable BLOCKED receipt when apply lacks a lease.
does_not_prove: Live Herdr API cleanup in this slice.
Global cleanup of arbitrary non-Tau Herdr resources.
Deletion of Git worktrees or local proof artifacts.
That a workspace lease came from an external human approval system.
next_recommended_route: Continue P1 with approval-packet hash validation or evidence-validator expansion for provider session/readiness receipts.
